name: ncat
title: ncat
description:
NMAP netcat reimplementation
ncat is a reimplementation of Netcat by the NMAP project, providing
most of the features present in the original implementations, along
with some new features such as IPv6 and SSL support. Port scanning
support has been removed.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use ncat because NMAP netcat reimplementation.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use ncat as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `ncat [OPTIONS...] [hostname] [port]` as documented upstream for ncat.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `ncat example.org 8080` as documented upstream for ncat.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `ncat -l 8080` as documented upstream for ncat.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `ncat --sh-exec "ncat example.org 80" -l 8080 --keep-open` as documented upstream for ncat.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair ncat with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `ncat --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install nmap. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `ncat [OPTIONS...] [hostname] [port]`. Further invocations from upstream: `ncat example.org 8080`; `ncat -l 8080`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: NMAP netcat reimplementation.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: NMAP netcat reimplementation.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install nmap
Install with: sudo apt install nmap
